home.title: Portfolio
home.intro: Hello, I'm a developer. This site will be completed soon.

presentation.name: CORNU Thomas
presentation.bio:
Full-stack developer with 3 years of experience in Angular and Java Spring Boot, working on the backend (REST APIs, databases, security) and the frontend (dynamic interfaces). 

 Curious, rigorous, and independent, I get involved in demanding projects by focusing on code quality (unit tests, CI with GitLab), performance, and user experience.

projects.title: Projects
projects.v2Hint: Click to open details in a window

dynatrust.title: Dynatrust
dynatrust.description: GRC software developed during my internship at DYNATRUST, the goal was to develop the SaaS solution aiming to establish a management compliant with international standards and norms in cybersecurity.
dynatrust.tags: Java, Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, Docker, Kubernetes, Python, GitLab CI/CD
dynatrust.detailBody:
For 3 years, I worked as a full-stack Java / Angular developer at the Dynatrust start-up.

My main mission was to design the GRC (Governance, Risks and Compliance) SaaS application, a platform allowing to simplify and secure the cybersecurity compliance of organizations, suppliers and clients, through different standards such as ISO 27001 or GDPR.

During this experience, I was able to develop my skills continuously over these three years, developing a strong ability to adapt to technical environments and evolving needs of the product. I worked in various work contexts: in a duo with the CTO, within a development team supervised by an engineering manager, as well as in a role of accompaniment of junior profiles (trainees), which allowed me to strengthen my skills in transmission and pedagogy.

I thus actively participated in the development of the application on the entire technical stack. On the back-end side, I worked on a Java-based architecture based on Spring Cloud, particularly using a microservices system using Netflix Eureka. On the front-end side, I designed and developed many components under Angular, strengthening my mastery of modern architectures as well as good development practices (modularity, reusability, maintainability).

I also contributed to the integration of generative AI features, using Python and the LangChain library, which allowed me to explore new use cases around automation and intelligent data processing.

On the technical level, this experience allowed me to become autonomous on the entire development chain, including versioning tools like Git, DevOps environments with Docker, as well as relational and non-relational database management (MySQL, MongoDB, PostgreSQL).

musculia.title: Musculia
musculia.description: Web application for personalized muscle training generation using AI, adapted to each user.
musculia.tags: Java, Spring Boot, MySQL, Python, AI, Docker, Angular
musculia.detailBody: Musculia details: problem, solution, architecture, AI, outcomes.

ytechUnion.title: YtechUnion
ytechUnion.description: Web agency creating portfolio sites for student and professional projects. Development of sites from the mockup to hosting.
ytechUnion.tags: React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, TypeScript, Figma
ytechUnion.detailBody:
During the Ydays project of my last year of MASTER, I participated as project lead and web devops at the YtechUnion project.

YTechUnion is a student project aiming to help students create their own websites. The initiative is led by a motivated and passionate team in web development, with the mission to facilitate the creation of portfolio sites for students and their projects.

We have realized three main projects with different approaches according to the needs of the clients.

LearnCyber: Project realized from A to Z in the absence of a provided mockup by the client. We accompanied the team throughout the process, from the definition of needs to the online launch of the site.

Propulse: The client provided us with a detailed mockup, so we mainly ensured technical integration while respecting the graphic requirements.

Narcisse: Hybrid project with a partial mockup. We worked closely with the client to adjust the design and complete the missing parts.

We initially considered creating a template of mockups to facilitate the design process, but this idea was not implemented in the end.

craft2give.title: Craft2Give
craft2give.description: Ingestion, transformation, and load jobs into a warehouse with scheduling and monitoring.
craft2give.detailBody:
During a student project at Ynov, I participated in a charitable initiative in favor of the SPA as a Front-End developer.

The main objective of this project was to organize a live on the Twitch platform to collect funds for the association, while raising awareness of the public to the animal cause. To enrich the experience and encourage donations, my team and I designed and developed a Minecraft module allowing viewers to interact in real time with the game through their contributions.

Within this project, I was primarily responsible for the graphic and communication part. I notably designed and developed the dedicated website for the event, ensuring a clear, attractive and adapted interface to the target audience.

This project allowed me to strengthen my technical skills in Front-End development, but also to work on aspects related to user experience, teamwork and the management of a real-impact project.

At the end of the live, we managed to collect more than 500€, entirely refunded to the association, contributing concretely to supporting its actions.

story.title: Story
story.carouselHint: Auto-advance every {seconds} seconds — click a card to select it.

bts.title: BTS SIO — option SLAM
bts.body:
When I obtained my Bac S, I decided to pursue my post-baccalaureate studies in the field of computer science.

This choice was imposed on me after my discovery of this field during my years of high school, particularly through the learning of the Python and HTML/CSS languages. I therefore chose to join the BTS SIO, option SLAM (IT Services for Organizations – Software Solutions and Application Services).

During this two-year training, I had the opportunity to discover the basics of web development and networks, particularly through the training given and the projects realized. Furthermore, I had the opportunity to carry out two internships (2 x 6 weeks) in the INFORMANET company, a company specializing in computer repair and maintenance. The objective of these internships was to familiarize me with the world of work and professional life while contributing to the realization of a concrete project. In the framework of these internships, my objective was to develop a web application allowing to record a repair or maintenance quote for a client. He wanted to go from a paper database to a digital database. I therefore developed this application using PHP and MySQL.

In parallel with these internships, I also participated in several academic projects during my two years of training. These projects allowed me to discover and practice different programming languages: Python, JavaScript, Java, C#, PHP and SQL.

They also allowed me to understand teamwork in a development context, as well as to familiarize myself with different project management methodologies, such as the cascade and agile approaches.

ynov.title: Ynov Campus
ynov.body:
After obtaining my BTS, I chose to pursue my studies in a more professional direction by integrating a work-study curriculum. This is in this framework that I joined the Lille Ynov Campus to follow a Bachelor in computer science.

During this year of Bachelor, in parallel with my work-study, the teaching is mainly based on the realization of projects, whether in team or autonomously, through different learning modules. This training allowed me to discover and practice several technologies and frameworks such as React, Angular and Node.js.

From my entry into Bachelor, I already had the objective of pursuing until the Master Developer Informatique at Lille Ynov Campus, still in work-study.

It is mainly during this Master that I was able to consolidate my technical and professional skills, particularly through more concrete projects such as my final project "Musculia" as well as my project Ydays "YtechUnion". These experiences allowed me to consolidate my achievements and develop advanced skills in web development.

The whole of this path led me to the acquisition of my RNCP level Master – Expert in web development diploma.

alternance.title: Work-study
alternance.body:
Parallel to my years at Lille Ynov Campus, I occupied a position of Full-stack Developer at the DYNATRUST start-up. My main mission consisted in contributing to the development of the GRC SaaS solution of DYNATRUST, aiming to ensure a management compliant with international standards and norms in cybersecurity.

I was recruited in December 2022 by the two founders, the CEO Matthieu Paganon and the CTO Thibault Denoyelle, as the first recruitment of this young start-up. During these three years, the development team has evolved several times with the arrival of other work-study students, trainees as well as an Engineering Manager.

Throughout my work-study, I was able to develop my skills in many areas offered by DYNATRUST. On the technical level, I worked on different languages and technologies such as Java Spring Boot, Angular and Python. I also participated in the implementation of software tests and the use of versioning tools such as Git.

On the methodological level, I applied the Agile practices, particularly Scrum and Kanban, in the framework of the projects of the team. In parallel, I also strengthened my knowledge in cybersecurity, particularly on subjects such as governance, risk management, compliance as well as the ISO 27001 standard.

This experience allowed me to realize many human and professional encounters, while being trained in an exacting environment. It helped me to become a more rigorous, autonomous and professional developer.

music.title: Music & festivals
music.body: Passionate about rap, French and American, I’m interested in both current and past music scenes. I enjoy attending concerts and festivals to discover new artists.

running.title: Running
running.body: I’ve been practicing sports since childhood (football, basketball, tennis, table tennis), I now dedicate myself to running, with personal goals and participation in official races (10 km in Lille in 52 min 27).

gaming.title: Gaming
gaming.body: Amateur of video games, retro as well as current, I’ve been playing different types of games since childhood, solo and multiplayer, and also appreciate sharing moments with friends during evenings.

travel.title: Travel
travel.body: Although not particularly interested in travel during my youth, I’ve developed a real interest in discovering new cities and different countries, in Europe as well as internationally, including a recent stay in Canada.

nba.title: NBA
nba.body: Despite a low talent for basketball, I have a marked interest in the NBA, both for the spectacle of the matches — despite the hours — and for the richness of its history and its players.

cooking.title: Cooking
cooking.body: In line with my practice of sport, it is important for me to have a good diet. I also enjoy taking the time to cook and discover different types of cuisine.

other.description: RPA, security, and skills that span several layers.
